title: robinia pseudoacacia subsp. tortuosa
intro: Robinia pseudoacacia subsp. tortuosa, also known as the twisted black locust, is a subspecies of the black locust tree, characterized by its unique twisted branches and compound leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce pain and inflammation. The health benefits of Robinia pseudoacacia subsp. tortuosa include its ability to reduce fever, improve digestion, and even help manage stress and anxiety. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
